articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

articles.headline: Martínez: Cristiano Ronaldo sets the example for Portugal

article.headline: Brazil fight back for draw in 3-3 thriller with Spain
article.description: Brazil's Lucas Paqueta scored a penalty deep in added time to rescue a fortunate 3-3 draw with Spain in an ill-tempered friendly at the Santiago Bernabeu stadium on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Yamal, Endrick star in thrilling Brazil-Spain draw

images.name: What to make of Spain's and Brazil's 3-3 draw?
images.caption: Luis Garcia analyses the high-scoring 3-3 draw between Spain and Brazil.

article.story:
Teenagers Lamine Yamal and Endrick took the spotlight as Brazil drew 3-3 with Spain on Tuesday in the "One Skin" international friendly that was set up to highlight the fight against racism after a series of insults aimed at the Real Madrid forward Vinícius Júnior.

Vinícius was relatively quiet in a match in which Brazil equalized with a penalty converted by Lucas Paquetá in stoppage time after Rodri's spot kick in 87th had put Spain ahead.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Brazil had rallied from two goals down with an equaliser scored by Vinícius' future Madrid teammate Endrick, the 17-year-old Palmeiras player who had made his scoring debut with Brazil just three days previous.

Spain was led by 16-year-old Barcelona forward Yamal, who helped set up all three goals for the hosts and received a standing ovation from the crowd at Madrid's Santiago Bernabeu Stadium when he was substituted near the end of the match.

The international friendly came a day after Vinícius broke down in tears while talking about the continued racist insults that he is subjected to in Spain, saying he was losing his desire to keep playing but pledged to continue his fight against racism.

Vinícius was loudly applauded when he was substituted in the 71st. However, the 23-year-old Brazilian, the Seleçao's captain on Tuesday, received some of the loudest jeers when his name was introduced ahead of the match.

Brazilian players went onto the field wearing black jackets launched by the Brazilian soccer federation ahead of the friendly accompanied with the slogan "One Skin, One Identity," in support of Vinícius' fight.

Vinícius, who has been accused of provoking fans and opponents, got into verbal altercations with Spanish players, including from the bench after leaving the match. He also shoved Spain defender Aymeric Laporte from behind while play was stopped.

The friendly game was set up by Spanish and Brazilian soccer officials amid the uproar that followed the racist insults aimed at Vinícius in a Spanish league game in Valencia 10 months ago. Many believed the incident would be a turning point for soccer in Spain, but another wave of racist abuse targeting Vinícius has followed.

While most of the attention was on Vinícius, it was Yamal threatening the most with his speed and quick moves to get past Brazilian defenders.

Rodri put Spain ahead by converting a 13th-minute penalty kick after Yamal made a run through the right side and was brought down by João Gomes inside the area.

Vinícius had Brazil's first shot on goal in the 17th but it was easily saved by Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón.

Dani Olmo was set up by Yamal before he added to Spain's lead in the 36th after a couple of crafty moves inside the area, including a ball between the legs of Brazil defender Beraldo.

Rodrigo pulled Brazil closer in the 40th after a mistake by Simón, who gave the ball away and left the Madrid forward with a shot into an open net.

Endrick, who will arrive in Spain at the end of the season, equalized but was later jeered when he gave the ball away to allow Paquetá to take the late penalty kick that gave Brazil the equalizer.

Vinícius went to defend Endrick from the bench after Spanish players loudly complained because of a hard foul by his future teammate late in the match.

Spain, preparing for the upcoming European Championship in Germany, were coming off a 1-0 loss to Colombia on Friday in London.

Vinícius has been subjected to multiple racist insults since arriving in Spain six years ago. He complained on Monday about the lack of punishment for perpetrators and asked for help from FIFA, UEFA and other soccer institutions in the fight against racism.
